name: Stir-Fried Chicken Shreds
recipeInstructions:
1. CUT THE CHICKEN INTO VERY THIN SHREDS and combine these with the egg white, salt, and cornstarch in a bowl. Mix well and chill in the refrigerator for about 20 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, trim the bean sprouts, finely shred the snow peas lengthwise, and shred or slice the water chestnuts.
3. HEAT THE OIL IN A WOK OR LARGE SKILLET until it is almost smoking. Add the chicken and STIR-FRY QUICKLY for 1 minute.
4. DRAIN THE CHICKEN in a colander or sieve immediately. POUR OFF THE OIL, leaving 1 tablespoon in the wok (the rest of the oil may be saved when cooled for future cooking with chicken).
5. REHEAT THE WOK AND STIR-FRY THE VEGETABLES for 2 minutes.
6. RETURN THE DRIED CHICKEN to the pan, stir to mix well, and add the salt. GIVE THE MIXTURE A FEW MORE STIRS before turning it onto a warm serving platter.
